subject: Geography
title: Icebergs
description: A short lesson to help you apply Icebergs.

hook.text: In 2021, an iceberg the size of Majorca broke off Antarctica—one of the largest ever recorded.

buildup.text: Icebergs calve from glaciers and ice shelves, floating north into shipping lanes.

discovery.text: Only about 10% of an iceberg sits above water. The rest lurks invisibly below the surface.

twist.text: The Titanic struck a relatively small iceberg—just 30 meters above water. It sank in under 3 hours.

climax.text: Satellites now track thousands of icebergs, but smaller 'growlers' remain a constant hazard.

punchline.text: What you see is the smallest part. The danger is below.

quiz.question: How much of an iceberg is visible above water?
